mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate accelerates rapidly, it signals an impending demand shock that forces equity risk premiums higher, depressing S&P 500 prices within a week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the trigger fails to predict a drop in forward returns exceeding 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with robust payroll growth indicate synchronized consumer spending and business investment, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and lifting equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or periods with high HOUST and high PAYEMS yield negative SPX returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained expansion, industrial production growth directly translates to margin expansion for cyclical equities, as firms cannot easily substitute capacity, leading to positive forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.0% quarter-over-quarter, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained real GDP growth expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, providing a fundamental floor that supports positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth and 60-day forward SPX returns show zero correlation (Pearson r < 0.05) over a 5-year sample, or negative GDP quarters do not coincide with negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index breaches a high inflation threshold, the market prices in prolonged central bank tightening, which compresses valuation multiples and drives negative equity returns within a week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds 230, or the threshold trigger fails to generate a statistically significant drop in 5-day returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising payroll employment and a stable unemployment rate indicates a structural labor shortage that accelerates wage inflation, squeezing corporate margins and depressing equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models, or periods of rising PAYEMS with flat UNRATE yield positive SPX returns more than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a credit tightening cycle, a contraction in residential mortgage debt outstanding directly reduces housing demand and consumer wealth, leading to negative equity returns over a weekly horizon as risk appetite evaporates.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not negatively correlated with declines in RSAFS, or the signal fails to predict downward price movement in > 55% of contraction episodes.
